top_stories.label: Top Stories
top_stories.ai_summary: The evening is dominated by Trump's Iran cabinet huddle — moved from Camp David to the White House on weather grounds — as a 60-day ceasefire-and-Hormuz deal hangs by a thread after fresh US self-defence strikes. Washington also tightened the screws on trade partners, giving the EU a July 4 deadline before tariffs jump to 25% and floating new Brazil duties. In US politics, Texas AG Ken Paxton ousted longtime Senator John Cornyn in a brutal GOP runoff, while Britain and parts of Europe baked in record May heat above 35°C.

stories.headline: Trump moves Iran cabinet meeting from Camp David to White House as deal teeters
stories.summary: Trump cancelled his planned Camp David cabinet session today, citing bad weather, and gathered top officials at the White House instead. The talks centre on a 60-day ceasefire under which Iran would clear mines in the Strait of Hormuz, the US would lift its naval blockade, and oil exports would resume — but US strikes on Iranian launch sites this week have rattled the negotiations.

stories.headline: Texas AG Ken Paxton defeats Senator John Cornyn in GOP runoff
stories.summary: Attorney General Ken Paxton beat long-time incumbent Senator John Cornyn in the Texas Republican Senate runoff, in one of the most-watched primary contests of the cycle. Paxton will now face Democrat state Rep. James Talarico in November in a race that could shift the Senate balance.

stories.headline: Trump gives EU July 4 deadline or tariffs jump to 25% on cars and trucks
stories.summary: Trump said he will raise tariffs on EU-made cars and light trucks from 15% to 25% unless the bloc ratifies its trade pact with Washington by July 4. The threat ratchets up pressure on European capitals already negotiating under last year's Scotland-deal framework.

stories.headline: US opens public comment process for lower China tariffs under new Board of Trade
stories.summary: US Trade Representative Jamieson Greer said a Federal Register notice will be issued shortly inviting American businesses and the public to suggest which Chinese goods should qualify for tariff cuts. The move follows the Trump-Xi summit in Beijing that chartered a joint Board of Trade.

stories.headline: London hits 35°C as European heatwave smashes May records
stories.summary: London notched back-to-back days above 35°C (95°F), with government heat warnings issued across the UK and parts of Western Europe. The figures shattered May temperature records and prompted health agencies to urge the elderly and vulnerable indoors during peak afternoon hours.

stories.headline: Brazil's Lula meets Trump as new Section 301 tariffs loom
stories.summary: Brazilian President Lula da Silva sat down with Trump at the White House in a bid to reset relations and head off fresh tariffs. A 10% surcharge on Brazilian exports is set to expire in July, but a Section 301 probe could replace it with steeper, targeted duties on items including ethanol.

singapore.label: Singapore
singapore.ai_summary: It's a wallet-focused day at home: COE Category B prices climbed past S$129,500 in the latest bidding exercise — bad news for anyone planning to buy a bigger car — while HDB confirmed about 6,900 BTO flats for the June launch, half of them under tighter Plus or Prime resale rules. On the health front, NEA flagged the start of peak dengue season with eastern clusters being targeted for vector control. Weather is calm for the rest of the week with only a 30% rain chance, a welcome break after a wet first half of May.

stories.headline: COE Category B premium jumps to S$129,501 in second May bidding
stories.summary: Premiums for larger cars (Category B, above 1,600cc or 130bhp) rose by S$3,265 to S$129,501 in the second COE bidding exercise of May. Commercial vehicle Category C also spiked S$4,744 to S$92,223 — analysts blame strong post-Car Expo demand and tight quotas, with no relief expected for the rest of 2026.

stories.headline: HDB to launch 6,900 BTO flats in June across seven projects
stories.summary: HDB confirmed the June 2026 Build-to-Order exercise will offer around 6,900 flats spread across Ang Mo Kio, Bishan, Bukit Merah, Sembawang and Woodlands. About half of the supply falls under the Plus or Prime tiers, which carry tighter resale and subsidy clawback rules.

stories.headline: NEA: Singapore enters peak dengue season, eastern clusters under vector control
stories.summary: NEA urged residents to stay vigilant as Singapore enters its peak dengue period. Year-to-date cases have reached around 1,200, with active clusters in eastern districts triggering ramped-up vector control sweeps. MOH is reminding residents to check the FightDengue app and remove stagnant water at home.

stories.headline: Weather: mostly clear week ahead, 30% chance of afternoon thunderstorms
stories.summary: After a soggy first half of May, the Meteorological Service forecasts a mostly clear stretch through early June, with only a 30% chance of brief afternoon thunderstorms. No active heavy rain or flood warnings are in force tonight.

stories.headline: Singapore among countries hosting quarantined ex-MV Hondius passengers
stories.summary: WHO data show Singapore is one of 12-plus countries where former passengers of the MV Hondius cruise are being monitored or were briefly hospitalised after the Andes hantavirus cluster on board. MOH's standing advice is to seek care if any returning traveller develops fever or shortness of breath within six weeks.

wars.label: Wars & Conflicts
wars.ai_summary: All three active fronts escalated at once today: Russia announced a fresh wave of strikes on Kyiv decision-making centres drawing UN alarm, the US-Iran ceasefire is described as on 'massive life support' after another round of US self-defence strikes, and Israel pounded Lebanon with 120 air raids killing at least 31 people while Defence Minister Katz announced a panel to advance 'voluntary emigration' from Gaza and confirmed the killing of Hamas military chief Mohammed Odeh.

theatres.name: Russia–Ukraine War
theatres.status: Escalating — Russia signals fresh strikes on Kyiv

timeline.event: Russia launches full-scale invasion of Ukraine across northern, eastern, and southern fronts.
Ukraine retakes Kharkiv region in a lightning counter-offensive.
Long-anticipated Ukrainian summer counter-offensive begins; gains in the south stall against deep Russian defensive lines.
Russia regains initiative; gradual advances in Donetsk; Western military aid is repeatedly stalled in US Congress.
Frontlines largely frozen along Donbas axis; drone warfare dominates with daily strikes deep into both countries.
Russia registers a net loss of 69 sq mi over the 4-week window; Ukraine continues to claw back small gains.
Ukrainian drone strike on Russian-occupied Snizhne (Donetsk) kills at least 65 Russian drone-pilot cadets and an instructor.
Russia carries out one of its heaviest aerial bombardments of Kyiv since the war began.
Moscow announces plans to strike Ukrainian 'decision-making centres' in Kyiv; UN Secretary-General Guterres says he is 'deeply concerned'.

theatres.name: US/Israel–Iran (Strait of Hormuz)
theatres.status: Ceasefire on 'massive life support' — peace deal under negotiation

timeline.event: Israel and the US launch coordinated war on Iran; Iranian Supreme Leader Ali Khamenei is killed in a strike.
'Twelve-Day War' devastates Iranian air defences and nuclear infrastructure; Iran retaliates with missile and drone barrages on Israel and US bases.
Fragile US-brokered ceasefire takes hold; Iran begins mining Strait of Hormuz.
US and Iran exchange fire in the Strait of Hormuz; each side blames the other.
Trump says ceasefire is on 'massive life support' after rejecting Tehran's latest proposal.
Trump claims a deal reopening the Strait of Hormuz is 'largely negotiated'.
US Central Command conducts 'self-defense' strikes on Iranian missile launch sites and boats in southern Iran.

IRGC declares 'legitimate' right to retaliate; Trump convenes cabinet at the White House (moved from Camp David due to weather) to chart next steps; IDF Chief Eyal Zamir says most of Iran's military capabilities are destroyed and its nuclear programme set back 'by years'.

theatres.name: Israel–Lebanon / Gaza
theatres.status: Active — heavy Israeli strikes on Lebanon; Hamas military chief killed in Gaza

timeline.event: Hamas-led attack on southern Israel triggers war in Gaza; Hezbollah opens northern front in solidarity.
Israel escalates against Hezbollah, killing leader Hassan Nasrallah; ground operations begin in southern Lebanon.
First Israel-Hezbollah ceasefire signed but repeatedly violated.
Israel resumes large-scale bombing of Lebanon; new phase of war begins (later termed '2026 Lebanon war').
US brokers a second Israel-Hezbollah ceasefire — Netanyahu publicly opposes ending the war.
Israel conducts more than 120 air raids on Lebanon — the sharpest single-day escalation since March; 31 killed and 40 wounded across the south and Bekaa Valley.
IDF strike in Gaza City kills Hamas military wing chief Mohammed Odeh; Defence Minister Israel Katz announces a panel to advance 'voluntary emigration' of Gaza residents.

hantavirus.label: Hantavirus Outbreak
hantavirus.ai_summary: The MV Hondius Andes hantavirus cluster has stabilised at 13 cases and 3 deaths as of yesterday, with all passengers having disembarked in Rotterdam on 18 May. Former passengers are being monitored across 12+ countries including Singapore, but WHO maintains the public risk remains low. The bigger global health story tonight is the US measles outbreak crossing 1,950 confirmed cases — putting elimination status at risk for the first time in a generation.

stories.headline: MV Hondius outbreak: 13 cases, 3 deaths confirmed as of 26 May
stories.summary: ECDC's latest update puts the MV Hondius Andes hantavirus cluster at 13 cases (11 confirmed, 2 probable) and 3 deaths, of which 2 are confirmed. The Dutch-flagged ship completed disembarkation in Rotterdam on 18 May after a Tenerife stop. Public-health follow-up continues in 12+ countries.

stories.headline: WHO: 'This is not another COVID' — risk to wider public stays low
stories.summary: WHO Director-General Tedros reiterated that the wider public risk from the Andes hantavirus cluster is low and that travellers should not panic. The agency is coordinating contact tracing with 12+ countries including Australia, Canada, Singapore, France, Germany and the US.

stories.headline: Andes hantavirus: how cruise-ship rodent exposure likely seeded the cluster
stories.summary: Researchers tracing the MV Hondius outbreak believe rodents that boarded the ship during its Patagonia leg are the most likely source. Andes hantavirus is unique among hantaviruses in that limited person-to-person transmission has been documented, which is why monitoring of former passengers continues for the full incubation window.

stories.headline: US measles cases hit 1,952 — elimination status at risk
stories.summary: CDC tallies show 1,952 confirmed US measles cases for the year through 21 May, with 93% tied to 29 active outbreaks. Officials warn summer travel could push numbers higher and that the US may lose the WHO 'elimination' label when status is reviewed in November.

cybersecurity.label: CyberSecurity
cybersecurity.ai_summary: The cycle's headline incident remains the Canvas/Instructure breach — 275 million education records and 9,000 institutions hit by ShinyHunters — now reportedly settled via ransom payment. Active exploitation is the other theme: CISA's deadline to patch a Drupal SQL injection hits tonight, a Ghost CMS SQL injection (CVE-2026-26980) is being mass-exploited for ClickFix attacks, and Cisco's Catalyst SD-WAN auth-bypass (CVE-2026-20182) is in the wild. Google also disclosed the first known AI-generated 2FA-bypass zero-day, a milestone in attacker tooling.

stories.headline: Instructure reportedly pays ransom to ShinyHunters to stop Canvas data leak
stories.summary: Instructure has reportedly reached an extortion agreement with ShinyHunters to prevent the public release of 3.65 TB of data stolen from its Canvas LMS, an incident affecting roughly 9,000 institutions and 275 million records on students, teachers and staff. The attackers exploited an unspecified flaw in the Free-for-Teacher support ticket system.

stories.headline: Ghost CMS SQL injection CVE-2026-26980 exploited in mass ClickFix campaign
stories.summary: Attackers are mass-exploiting a critical SQL injection vulnerability in Ghost CMS (CVE-2026-26980) to inject malicious JavaScript that funnels visitors into ClickFix social-engineering attack flows. Site owners running self-hosted Ghost are urged to patch immediately and audit content for injected scripts.

stories.headline: CISA deadline tonight: federal agencies must patch exploited Drupal flaw
stories.summary: US federal agencies have until end-of-day Wednesday (27 May) to remediate an actively exploited Drupal SQL injection vulnerability added to CISA's Known Exploited Vulnerabilities catalog. Private-sector Drupal admins are urged to use the same deadline.

stories.headline: Cisco Catalyst SD-WAN auth bypass (CVE-2026-20182) under active attack
stories.summary: Cisco's Catalyst SD-WAN Controller carries a critical authentication-bypass vulnerability (CVE-2026-20182) that is being exploited in the wild to gain administrative privileges. CISA ordered federal agencies to patch affected devices by 17 May; many enterprise environments are still unpatched.

stories.headline: Google: first known AI-developed zero-day 2FA bypass spotted in the wild
stories.summary: Google Threat Intelligence Group disclosed what it believes is the first AI-developed zero-day exploit observed in real attacks — a Python script that bypasses two-factor authentication on a popular open-source sysadmin tool. The tooling marks a step-change in how attackers may generate exploits at scale.

stories.headline: Chrome zero-day CVE-2026-5281 patched — WebGPU/Dawn use-after-free
stories.summary: Google pushed an emergency Chrome update fixing 21 flaws, including a high-severity zero-day use-after-free in Dawn, the WebGPU implementation (CVE-2026-5281). Restart browsers tonight to pick up the fix.

stories.headline: CISA adds 7 vulnerabilities to KEV — Microsoft Defender flaws included
stories.summary: CISA added seven entries to its Known Exploited Vulnerabilities catalog on 20 May, including CVE-2026-41091 (Microsoft Defender elevation of privilege) and CVE-2026-45498 (Microsoft Defender denial of service), plus several older Microsoft and Adobe flaws still being abused.
